text.title: AI-Feature Observability Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every LLM-feature observability setup MUST satisfy on launch day. Hard rules; "we'll add it later" is the dominant failure pattern this list prevents.

rule.statement: The feature MUST emit metrics for all four pillars on launch day: latency (p50/p95/p99 in ms), cost-per-request (in real currency with model+token breakdown), quality_score (from eval set or human grade), drift_signal (input-token mean, refusal_rate, or semantic-cluster drift). Shipping with three pillars is REJECTED at launch review.
rule.rationale: Missing a pillar is the dominant root cause of "we didn't know the model was worse" postmortems. The four together cover symptoms (latency, cost), outcomes (quality), and leading indicators (drift). Three pillars always misses one class of incident.

rule.statement: SLO bands MUST be set from 1-2 weeks of pre-launch baseline traffic — never from intuition or vendor defaults. The default SLO assignment is: p95_latency < 2x baseline p95; cost_per_request < 1.25x baseline mean; quality_score >= baseline_mean - 1.5*sigma; drift_signal alert when KL or PSI > 0.2 over baseline distribution.

rationale.source: Google SRE workbook Ch.4; statistical process control

rule.rationale: SLO bands chosen by intuition pass even when the system degrades. Baseline-derived bands fail when the system is actually worse. The 2x / 1.25x / 1.5-sigma defaults are conservative starting points that get tightened as the team learns the production shape.

rule.statement: The cost pillar MUST be expressed in real currency (USD by default) per request, NOT in tokens. Token counts are stored as inputs to cost computation but the dashboard metric is currency. Cost MUST break down by model name and by direction (input tokens, cached input, output tokens, thinking tokens where applicable).

rationale.source: Faion product rule `feedback_no_token_pricing` + stakeholder reporting practice

rule.rationale: Token counts read different across models (Sonnet vs Opus vs Haiku); currency is the only stakeholder-readable signal. Token-only dashboards are the dominant pattern that hides 4-5x cost increases when a model swap inflates output tokens silently.

rule.statement: Every feature MUST name an `eval_set_owner` (single human accountable) AND a cadence (daily or weekly run). Eval-set runs older than 7 days are STALE; the quality_score metric MUST emit a `stale` tag on every observation older than the cadence. Eval sets without an owner are FORBIDDEN.
rule.rationale: Eval sets rot fast — production traffic drifts, examples become outdated, gold answers become wrong. Ownership + cadence is the only mechanism that keeps the eval set live. An unowned eval set is no signal.

rule.statement: The drift_signal pillar MUST declare its chosen drift definition explicitly: (a) input-token mean with EMA; (b) refusal_rate ratio; (c) semantic-cluster drift via embedding distance over a rolling window. Defaults: chat features use (b); RAG features use (c); long-context features use (a). Implicit "drift" with no definition is REJECTED.

rationale.source: ML monitoring literature; arXiv:2206.06672 drift metrics taxonomy

rule.rationale: "Drift" is ambiguous without a chosen metric. The three families catch different shifts (volume / model-policy / query-topic). Picking one per feature is the discipline that makes alerts actionable.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.

example.title: Four-pillar dashboard at launch
example.code: Panel 1: latency_ms p50=420 p95=1180 p99=2340 (SLO: p95 < 2000) Panel 2: cost_usd mean=0.0042 p95=0.018 (SLO: mean < 0.005) Panel 3: quality_score daily=4.21 / 5 (eval set 200) (SLO: > 4.10) Panel 4: refusal_rate 1.2% baseline 1.1% (PSI 0.04) (alert: PSI > 0.2)
example.note: r1 satisfied (four pillars), r3 satisfied (currency not tokens), r5 satisfied (refusal_rate drift declared with PSI). Bands from a 14-day baseline.

example.title: Tokens-as-cost dashboard
example.code: Panel 1: latency_ms p50=420 Panel 2: tokens_per_req mean=2400 p95=8400 Panel 3: (no quality) Panel 4: drift (label exists, no metric attached)
example.note: r1, r3, r5 violations. Tokens hide a 4x cost spike if model swaps from Haiku to Opus mid-week. Quality absent. Drift undefined.
